---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# tabexp

<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

The goal of tabexp is to create a table of births and exposure from a DHS-like birth history. It uses the same structure as the individual recode data file in DHS. You can use a data frame or a data table in R, or directly call a DHS Stata file from R.

## Installation

You can install the development version of tabexp like so:

``` r
# FILL THIS IN! HOW CAN PEOPLE INSTALL YOUR DEV PACKAGE?
```

## Example

This is a basic example which shows you how to solve a common problem:

```{r example}
library(tabexp)
tabexp(data = AMIR54FL)
```
